rationale: Both candidates converge on the same latent theme the ground truth reviewer raised (manager.py hardcodes a component list that duplicates existing per-pipeline metadata like _dit_modules), though neither cites the exact GT reference to the Qwen WA or _packed_modules_mapping, capping recall for both. X is tighter and adds a genuinely sharp second finding (text-encoder LoRA is a silent no-op via the same failure mode the PR itself describes), while Y pads with two overlapping variants of the same hardcoded-list critique (374/375) before adding a solid but more generic missing-test-coverage note. Both are well-grounded and actionable with concrete file:line references, but X's non-redundant, more insightful second point edges out Y's repetition.
